channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Osteoporosis drug shows potential against diseases caused by excess iron
item.description: Researchers at the University of São Paulo (USP) in Brazil have discovered that two osteoporosis drugs (etidronate and tiludronate) may combat disease...

item.title: A landmark 'evolutionary double-bind' strategy to overcome treatment resistance in prostate cancer
item.description: Many patients with metastatic cancers receive therapy that is initially highly effective, often resulting in complete remission. However, cancer cells...

item.title: Sex and menopause-based differences found in presentation of early Lyme disease
item.description: In a new Johns Hopkins Medicine study, researchers found that male and female patients with early Lyme disease present with different signs of the dis...

item.title: AI decodes tissue characteristics of type 2 diabetes
item.description: Researchers from several partner institutions of the German Center for Diabetes Research (DZD) have collaborated with international colleagues to deve...

item.title: Post-disaster prevalence of mental health problems can peak more than a decade later, says meta-analysis
item.description: After a disaster, population mental health generally improves, but an increase in mental health problems often follows, potentially peaking years late...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris challenges the conventional focus on sleep duration and makes the case that sleep timing and circadi...

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
item.description: Seed oils have become one of the most controversial topics in nutrition, especially after a 2024 University of South Florida study linked high omega-6...
